content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 1102 Q Street• Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
/ (916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-.0886 
Mark Bramfitt, P.E. 
Executive Officer 
January 25, 2017 
Sonoma Local Agency Formation Commission 
575 Administration Drive, Room 104A 
Santa Rosa, CA 95403-2888 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-17-289 
Dear Mr. Brarnfitt: 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of the Sonoma County Local 
Agency Formation Commission ("Sonoma County LAFCO") regarding the conflict of interest 
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Please note that we are only providing advice 
under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest 
prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest or Section l 090. 
Also, note that we are Ëot a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is 
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. 
QUESTION 
May Sonoma County LAFCO Commissioners Steve Herzberg and Rodney Moore 
participate in governmental decisions regarding an application seeking dissolution of the Palm 
Drive Health Care District ("District"), when dissolution of the District would eliminate a property 
tax they currently pay as residents who own real property within the District? 
CONCLUSION 
. No. Sonoma County LAFCO Commissioners Herzberg and Moore may not participate in 
governmental decisions regarding an application seeking dissolution of the District because there is 
a reasonably foreseeable material financial effec_t upon their financial interests. 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


FACTS 
File No. A-17-289 
Page No. 2 
You are the Executive Officer of the Sonoma County LAFCO and are seeking advice on 
behalf of two alternate members of the Sonoma County LAFCO. Steve Herzberg, an alternate 
public member, was selected to serve on the Sonoma County LAFCO by the balance of the 
Commission. Commissioner Herzberg is a resident and a landowner in Bodega Bay, California. 
Rodney Moore, an alternate Special District member, was elected to serve on the Sonoma County 
LAFCO by the boards of special districts within the County. Commissioner Moore is also a resident 
and landowner in Bodega say, California. 
The Sonoma County LAFCO adjudicated an application for detachment from the District in 
2016 and at that time the Sonoma County LAFCO sought guidance from the Commission. (Branfitt 
Advice Letter, No. A-16-222.) In the Branfitt Advice Letter we advised that a Sonoma County 
LAFCO Commissioner could not take part in governmental decisions regarding the detachment of 
parcels from the District. 
Currently, there is no active application seeking dissolution of the District, but there is a 
possibility that community members may seek to file one by means of a petition. Additionally, 
under changes to the Sonoma County LAFCO's governing regulations, the Commission may 
initiate dissolution proceedings unilaterally. 
If the District were dissolved, landowners within the District would continue to pay parcel 
taxes until District debts are discharged, but the taxes would eventually be discontinued. This 
benefit would accrue to all landowners within the District, but not all landowners within the 
County, as the District is a subset of the County. Both Commissioner Herzberg and Moore are 
landowners within the District. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using 
his or her position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. 
(Section 87103.) As previously analyzed in the Bran.fill Advice Letter, supra, decisions regarding 
the dissolution of the District would involve the repeal or modification of the current assessment on 
each of the Commissioners' properties, their interests are explicitly involved in the decisions and 
the financial effect of the decisions on the interests in the property is both foreseeable and material. 
Accordingly, the Commissioners may not make, participate, or use their official positons to 
influence the decisions. 


File No. A-1 7-289 
Page No. 3 
Moreover, you have not provided facts showing that the number of parcels affected in the 
District constitute a significant segment of the official's jurisdiction.2 Absent additional facts, 
Commissioners Herzberg and Moore may not invoke the public generally exception. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
SKB:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Brian G. Lau 
Assistant General Counsel 
By: 
Sukhi K. Brar 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division 
2 For purposes of the public generally exception, an official's "jurisdiction" is the geographical area in which 
the official's agency has jurisdiction, the geographical area the official was elected to represent, or the area to which the 
official's authority and duties are limited if not elected. (Section 82035; Regulation l 8703(d).) In this case, the 
Commissioners have not been elected to represent a limited geographical area. Accordingly, the Commissioners' 
jurisdiction for purposes of applying the public generally exception is the jurisdiction of the Sonoma Local Agency 
Fonnation Commission, which appears to be the County of Sonoma.
